name: TC-NFR-05: Delete User Account — Soft Delete Load Test
description: Validate that the PATCH /api/v1/users/{user_id} endpoint correctly handles 50 concurrent soft-delete (status deactivation) requests within the required response time and success rate thresholds, while ensuring deactivated users are no longer retrievable via the active users listing.
corresponding NFR: The /api/v1/users/{user_id} endpoint (PATCH) must process soft-delete requests (status: deactivated) with a maximum response time of 500 ms and a success rate of 98% or above under a simultaneous load of 50 virtual users. Deactivated users must not appear in subsequent GET /users/active responses.
preconditions: At least 50 test users exist in the system with status: active.
Each test user has a unique user_id pre-loaded in a parameterisation CSV.
Valid authorization Bearer tokens are available for all 50 test users.
No test user has pending transactions that would block deactivation.
The application and database services are running and accessible.
The test environment is isolated from production traffic.

metrics.metric: Data Integrity — deactivated users absent from /users/active

notes: Duration of 300 s and ramp-up of 30 s are proposed defaults — adjust per test plan. Each VU must consume a unique user_id and token from CSV to prevent duplicate-deactivation conflicts. A post-test assertion sampler must query /users/active and confirm none of the deactivated IDs are present (data integrity gate).
